articles.headline: Jim Cramer on MP Materials: “We’re Not Going to Let This One Go Away”
articles.summary: MP Materials Corp. (NYSE:MP) is one of the stocks Jim Cramer looked at. A caller asked whether Cramer considers the stock speculative at this point or sees long-term growth potential. In response, he said: No, I think it does have long-term… in part because the president has pretty much just said, you know what, we’re […]

articles.headline: MP Materials 10X Campus Investment Reshapes Valuation And Growth Story
articles.summary: MP Materials (NYSE:MP) plans to build a $1.25b rare earth magnet manufacturing campus in Northlake, Texas. The project, called the "10X" campus, is described as the largest rare earth magnet manufacturing site in U.S. history. The campus is backed by a long-term partnership with the U.S. Department of Defense and incentives from Texas and local authorities. MP Materials expects the development to create over 1,500 jobs and support American supply chain independence. MP Materials focuses on...

articles.headline: China's Rare Earth Dominance and the Strategic Failure of the West
articles.summary: REalloys (ALOY) and a small number of other North American companies are doing what the rest of the Western world failed to do for three decades: They are breaking China's "kill switch" on the U.S. defense supply chain. Companies mentioned in this release include: REalloys Inc. (ALOY), Vale S.A. (NYSE: VALE), Energy Fuels Inc. (NYSE American: UUUU), MP Materials Corp. (NYSE: MP), Critical Metals Corp. (NASDAQ: CRML), USA Rare Earth Inc. (NASDAQ: USAR)

articles.headline: Rare Earth Stocks Flash Buy Signals As Missiles Fly
articles.summary: Rare earth stocks including MP Materials and USA Rare Earth flashed buy signals on Monday as the war with Iran added to the urgency of rebuilding and expanding the U.S. missile arsenal.  MP and USAR offered early entry opportunities as they moved clear of key support levels.  The live hostilities could last for four weeks, President Donald Trump said on Sunday and indicated on Monday that the "big wave" of the attack against Iran is still to come.

articles.summary: MP Materials Corp. (NYSE:MP) is among the 12 Most Promising EV Battery Stocks to Buy Now. MP Materials Corp. (NYSE:MP) is among the most promising stocks. articles.summary: The Dallas-Fort Worth area site is part of the company's $500 million agreement with the Defense Department, prompting lawmakers to question the deal during a senate hearing.

articles.headline: MP Materials Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
articles.summary: MP Materials (NYSE:MP) executives told investors the company marked a “landmark year” in 2025, pointing to higher rare earth oxide output at Mountain Pass, progress toward commercial magnet production in Texas, and new long-term customer agreements as demand for NdPr accelerates. articles.headline: Stocks to Watch Friday Recap: Dell, Duolingo, Compass, CoreWeave
articles.summary: ↗️ Dell (DELL): The technology company reported a 39% leap in quarterly sales, boosted by its artificial-intelligence server business. Shares surged 22% Friday. ↘️ Duolingo (DUOL): The educational technology company said it expects a slowdown in bookings and revenue growth this year, as well as lower profitability.

articles.headline: Evaluating MP Materials (MP) After Recent Share Price Pullback And Conflicting Valuation Signals
articles.summary: MP Materials (MP) has drawn fresh attention after recent trading left the stock roughly 10% lower over the past month, even as its year to date return stands at about 9%. See our latest analysis for MP Materials. Recent trading has been choppy, with a 2.28% 1 day share price return and a 2.46% 7 day share price return sitting against a 10.09% 30 day share price decline. At the same time, the 1 year total shareholder return of very large magnitude signals that longer term momentum has been...

articles.headline: MP Materials posts Q4 profit, unveils $1.25B Texas magnet factory plans
articles.summary: MP Materials Corp (NYSE: MP) reported a profitable fourth quarter driven largely by a $51 million non-revenue Price Protection Agreement from the US Department of War, as the rare-earths miner also announced plans for a major new manufacturing campus in Texas. articles.headline: Does MP’s Texas 10X Magnet Campus and Pentagon Deal Change The Bull Case For MP (MP)?
articles.summary: MP Materials recently reported fourth-quarter 2025 sales of US$52.69 million and net income of US$9.43 million, while also confirming a more than US$1.25 billion investment to build its “10X” rare earth magnet manufacturing campus in Northlake, Texas. This combination of improved quarterly profitability and a fully integrated, government-backed U.S. magnet facility underlines MP’s evolving role as a core supplier to defense, semiconductor, and advanced manufacturing supply chains. Next,...
